CVE-2016-20013.title: sha256crypt and sha512crypt through 0.6 allow attackers to cause  ...
CVE-2016-20013.summary: sha256crypt and sha512crypt through 0.6 allow attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) because the algorithm's runtime is proportional to the square of the length of the password.

CVE-2021-3621.title: A flaw was found in SSSD, where the sssctl command was vulnerable ...
CVE-2021-3621.summary: A flaw was found in SSSD, where the sssctl command was vulnerable to shell command injection via the logs-fetch and cache-expire subcommands. This flaw allows an attacker to trick the root user into running a specially crafted sssctl command, such as via sudo, to gain root access. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to confidentiality, integrity, as well as system availability.

CVE-2022-4254.title: sssd: libsss_certmap fails to sanitise certificate data used in L ...
CVE-2022-4254.summary: sssd: libsss_certmap fails to sanitise certificate data used in LDAP filters

CVE-2023-3326.title: pam_krb5 authenticates a user by essentially running kinit with t ...
CVE-2023-3326.summary: pam_krb5 authenticates a user by essentially running kinit with the password, getting a ticket-granting ticket (tgt) from the Kerberos KDC (Key Distribution Center) over the network, as a way to verify the password. However, if a keytab is not provisioned on the system, pam_krb5 has no way to validate the response from the KDC, and essentially trusts the tgt provided over the network as being valid. In a non-default FreeBSD installation that leverages pam_krb5 for authentication and does not have a keytab provisioned, an attacker that is able to control both the password and the KDC responses can return a valid tgt, allowing authentication to occur for any user on the system.

CVE-2023-3758.title: A race condition flaw was found in sssd where the GPO policy is n ...
CVE-2023-3758.summary: A race condition flaw was found in sssd where the GPO policy is not consistently applied for authenticated users. This may lead to improper authorization issues, granting or denying access to resources inappropriately.
